QF.id: VII Introduction to Securities Broking Operations_99

question.text: For entities like insurance companies that are required to settle all transactions by delivery, brokers may allot how many trading codes per client?

answer.text: Up to two (one for buy and one for sell)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Brokers may allot up to two trading codes (for buy and sell separately) to investors required by regulations to settle by delivery only. Both codes map to the same UCC, ensuring separate treatment without netting. **The Trap:** This is an exception to the normal rule of one trading code per client. It applies only to entities that must take/give delivery per their regulations — not to ordinary investors. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series XII covers special trading code provisions. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Two trading codes prevent unintended netting. Without them, the system might treat a buy and sell as a square-off (non-delivery), which would violate the entity's regulatory requirement for delivery settlement. STT is also correctly determined based on delivery-only treatment.
